qa_pairs.question: What is biomolecular simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: Biomolecular simulation is the application of molecular dynamics simulations to biochemical research questions.

qa_pairs.question: What types of processes can be modelled using biomolecular simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: Processes that can be modelled include protein folding, drug binding, membrane transport, and conformational changes critical to protein function.

qa_pairs.question: How is biomolecular simulation related to computational chemistry?
qa_pairs.answer: Biomolecular simulation could be considered a sub-field of computational chemistry, but it is sufficiently specialized that there is a Biomolecular Simulations National Team supporting this area.

qa_pairs.question: Is there any overlap in software tools between biomolecular simulation and computational chemistry?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is some overlap of software tools between biomolecular simulation and computational chemistry.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find an annotated list of available software packages in computational chemistry?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find an annotated list of available software packages in computational chemistry by referring to the [[Computational chemistry]] section.

qa_pairs.question: Who authored the article titled "Biomolecular Simulation: A Computational Microscope for Molecular Biology"?
qa_pairs.answer: The article "Biomolecular Simulation: A Computational Microscope for Molecular Biology" was authored by Ron O. Dror, Robert M. Dirks, J.P. Grossman, Huafeng Xu, and David E. Shaw.

qa_pairs.question: In which publication and year was "Biomolecular Simulation: A Computational Microscope for Molecular Biology" published?
qa_pairs.answer: It was published in the ''Annual Review of Biophysics'', volume 41, pages 429-452, in 2012.

qa_pairs.question: What software packages for biomolecular simulation are available on our HPC resources?
qa_pairs.answer: The software packages available on our HPC resources include AMBER, GROMACS, NAMD, DL_POLY, HOOMD-blue, LAMMPS, OpenKIM, OpenMM, PLUMED, Rosetta, DSSP, and VMD.

qa_pairs.question: What is OpenKIM?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenKIM is the Knowledgebase of Interatomic Models.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of PLUMED?
qa_pairs.answer: PLUMED is a library for code development related to the calculation of free energy in molecular dynamics simulations.

qa_pairs.question: Where can Python packages useful for biomolecular simulation be found?
qa_pairs.answer: Python packages useful in the domain of biomolecular simulation/molecular dynamics can be found in our Wheelhouse.

qa_pairs.question: How are Python Wheels installed for biomolecular simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: Python Wheels can be installed within a virtual Python environment.

qa_pairs.question: Is the list of Python Wheels for biomolecular simulation exhaustive?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the provided list contains a selection of useful wheels, but it is not considered complete.

qa_pairs.question: What is ACPYPE used for?
qa_pairs.answer: ACPYPE: AnteChamber PYthon Parser interfacE is a tool to generate topologies for chemical compounds.

qa_pairs.question: What functionality does MDAnalysis provide?
qa_pairs.answer: MDAnalysis is an object-oriented Python library used to analyse trajectories from molecular dynamics (MD) simulations in many popular formats.

qa_pairs.question: What is MDTraj capable of?
qa_pairs.answer: MDTraj can read, write, and analyse MD trajectories with only a few lines of Python code, supporting a wide range of MD formats.

qa_pairs.question: What is Biopython?
qa_pairs.answer: Biopython is a set of freely available tools for biological computation.

qa_pairs.question: What is foyer used for in the context of biomolecular simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: Foyer is a package for atom-typing as well as applying and disseminating force fields.

qa_pairs.question: What is mBuild?
qa_pairs.answer: MBuild is a hierarchical, component-based molecule builder.

qa_pairs.question: What is mdsynthesis?
qa_pairs.answer: Mdsynthesis is a persistence engine for molecular dynamics data.

qa_pairs.question: What is nglview?
qa_pairs.answer: NGL Viewer (nglview) is a collection of tools for web-based molecular graphics.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of ParmEd?
qa_pairs.answer: ParmEd is a general tool for aiding in investigations of biomolecular systems using popular molecular simulation packages.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of simulations does PyRETIS specialize in?
qa_pairs.answer: PyRETIS is a Python library for rare event molecular simulations with an emphasis on methods based on transition interface sampling and replica exchange transition interface sampling.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check the available Python Wheels on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check the available Python Wheels on our clusters by consulting the [[Available_Python_wheels|list of available wheels]] or by using the [[Python#Listing_available_wheels|avail_wheels command]].

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I need additional Python packages or newer versions?
qa_pairs.answer: If you require additional Python packages or newer versions, please contact Support.

qa_pairs.question: Which team offers Molecular Dynamics workshops?
qa_pairs.answer: The Molecular Modelling and Simulation National Team offers Molecular Dynamics workshops.

qa_pairs.question: How will future Molecular Dynamics workshops be announced?
qa_pairs.answer: Future Molecular Dynamics workshops will be announced in our Newsletters.

qa_pairs.question: Is material available for self-study for Molecular Dynamics?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the workshop material is also available for self-study.

qa_pairs.question: What self-study topics are available for Molecular Dynamics?
qa_pairs.answer: Self-study topics include practical considerations for Molecular Dynamics, visualizing structures with VMD, running Molecular Dynamics with Amber on our clusters, and analysing Molecular Dynamics data with PYTRAJ.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find self-study material on running Molecular Dynamics with Amber on clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Material for running Molecular Dynamics with Amber on our clusters is available for self-study.

qa_pairs.question: What resource helps determine optimal conditions for Molecular Dynamics jobs on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: A Molecular Dynamics Performance Guide, created by a team at ACENET, can help you determine optimal conditions for Amber, GROMACS, NAMD, and OpenMM jobs on Alliance clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Who created the Molecular Dynamics Performance Guide for Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: A team at ACENET created the Molecular Dynamics Performance Guide for Alliance clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Which software jobs can the Molecular Dynamics Performance Guide help optimize?
qa_pairs.answer: The Molecular Dynamics Performance Guide can help you determine optimal conditions for Amber, GROMACS, NAMD, and OpenMM jobs.
